title: Fear & Hunger — World Overview
text: Fear & Hunger is a horror dungeon crawler set in the dark dungeons of fear and hunger beneath the Kingdom of Rondon, released December 2018. Four adventurers — Cahara (mercenary), D'arce (knight), Enki (dark priest), and Ragnvaldr (outlander) — delve into the depths, each with their own motives. The fortress acts as an ancient nexus for different planes of existence where old and new gods struggle for power. The game features lovecraftian horror elements, limb-targeting combat, and permanent consequences. The ancient city of Ma'habre lies beneath the dungeons, once home to the Old Gods and later the New Gods, now decaying and largely forgotten.

title: The Prophecy of Le'garde
text: Le'garde is a prophesied leader and captain of the Knights of the Midnight Sun, a mercenary group. Ancient prophecies foretold a figure who would unify the western world without violence. Le'garde's magnetic leadership drew followers including D'arce Cataliss. His success was attributed to a prophetic vision of a unified kingdom. Over time, the New God Nilvan came to believe Le'garde held the key to her eternal existence, visiting him in a dream and conceiving the Girl. Le'garde became obsessed with ancient texts and the occult, invading Oldegård for the Cube of the Depths. This aggression allowed Rondon to imprison him in the dungeons where Trortur tortured him.

title: Gods of Fear & Hunger
text: The pantheon includes Old Gods (Gro-goroth, Sylvian, God of the Depths, Rher, Vinushka) who created the world and have since departed. New Gods are former mortals who ascended using the secrets of Ma'habre: The Fellowship consists of Valteil (Enlightenment), Nilvan (Endless), Francóis (Domination), and the Tormented One. Ascended Gods include Alll-mer and the God of Fear and Hunger. Each New God embodies a distinct soul type that grants their power. Le'garde's plot involves encompassing all soul aspects (torment through torture, enlightenment through the Cube, endlessness through Nilvan, domination through his campaign) to become greater than any New God.

title: Ma'habre — The Ancient City of the Gods
text: Ma'habre (also Ma'havre) is the ancient city of the gods beneath the Dungeons of Fear and Hunger. Originally built to worship the Old Gods, it later became home to the New Gods who transformed it according to their whims. The grand libraries were created by Betel, a New God of Enlightenment. In the year 809, the Fellowship descended into the city and became New Gods themselves. By 1590, Ma'habre is decaying and forgotten, with the aged New Gods having slowly lost their powers. The Cube of the Depths is required to access Ma'habre and can also be used as a Beacon of the Depths to travel between the past (thriving Ma'habre) and present (dilapidated Ancient City).

title: The Four Protagonists — Motivations
text: Cahara is a mercenary from the Eastern Sanctuaries hired by Rondon to extract Le'garde — he seeks money to free his lover Celeste, a prostitute in indentured servitude. D'arce is a Rondon knight who left the kingdom's army disillusioned and joined the Knights of the Midnight Sun — she loves Le'garde and enters the dungeons to save him. Enki is a dark priest driven by his enlightened soul to pursue forbidden knowledge — he seeks the secrets of the dungeons and Ma'habre. Ragnvaldr is an outlander from Oldegård whose tribe was massacred by the Knights of the Midnight Sun — he seeks vengeance on Le'garde and to recover the Cube of the Depths stolen from his people.

title: The Throne of Ascension
text: The Throne of Ascension is located in the Golden Temple within Ma'habre. It is the mechanism by which mortals can achieve godhood. To access it, one must collect the four souls of the New Gods (Tormented, Endless, Enlightened, and Dominating) and defeat the Nameless guardian. Sitting on the throne transports the user to The Void where the final confrontation occurs. This is Le'garde's ultimate goal — to seize power greater than any New God by encompassing all aspects of divinity. Nosramus, the ancient alchemist, describes Le'garde's plan as not necessarily the right path but an interesting event to unfold.
